notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateOpaInstance, GetOpaInstance, ListOpaInstances, UpdateOpaInstance, DeleteOpaInstance, ChangeOpaInstanceCompartment, StartOpaInstance, StopOpaInstance, GetWorkRequest, ListWorkRequests, ListWorkRequestErrors, and ListWorkRequestLogs are present in v65.110.0. ListOpaInstances filters compartmentId, lifecycleState, displayName, and id, while create, update, and delete responses expose opc-work-request-id but do not return an OpaInstance body.

Pinned provider surfaces from the onboarding audit: oci_opa_opa_instance is the resource and singular data source, and oci_opa_opa_instances is the list data source. The reviewed runtime keeps using service-SDK GetWorkRequest polling and shared async status instead of provider helper waiters.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create stays explicit: when no OCI identifier is tracked, the runtime uses exact compartmentId plus displayName matching, ignores FAILED, DELETING, and DELETED summaries, and fails on duplicate exact-name matches instead of adopting arbitrarily.

Repo-authored mutation policy stays explicit: only displayName, description, freeformTags, and definedTags reconcile in place. ChangeOpaInstanceCompartment, StartOpaInstance, and StopOpaInstance remain out-of-scope auxiliary operations, while compartmentId, shapeName, consumptionModel, meteringType, IdcsAt, and isBreakglassEnabled stay replacement-only drift.

IdcsAt remains a create-time input only. OCI does not echo it back on OpaInstance, so the reviewed runtime intentionally normalizes it out of post-create parity checks instead of treating the missing value as replacement-required drift.

Reviewed lifecycle handling treats INACTIVE as a successful steady state alongside ACTIVE because the service exposes START/STOP auxiliaries that remain out of scope for the published runtime surface.
